footer.tagline: Auditable · End-to-End Encrypted · No Accounts Required · No Cookies · No Tracking
hero.caption: Open-source infrastructure you own entirely.
meta.description: Exchange contacts once, they update themselves. End-to-end encrypted, no accounts, open source.
meta.title: Vauchi - Privacy-First Contact Exchange
outro.caption: Exchange once. Stay connected — no matter what changes.
outro.footer: Auditable · End-to-End Encrypted · No Accounts Required
outro.heading: vauchi
scene1.heading: Your address book is a graveyard of dead contacts.
scene1.label: The Problem
scene2.heading: Meet in person. Scan. Connected.
scene2.label: The Problem
scene2.sub: No phone number or account needed
scene3.heading: Both phones now hold each other's encrypted contact card.
scene3.label: Contact Exchange
scene4.heading: When your details change, your friends' phones update automatically. No notifications, no noise.
scene4.label: ✓ Exchanged
scene5.heading: Switch social networks? Change email providers? Your contacts stay current.
scene5.label: Silent Updates
scene5.sub: update once — everyone sees the change
scene6.heading: Share work details with colleagues and home addresses with family — all from a single identity.
scene6.label: Platform Freedom
scene7.heading: No master passwords to lose. Regain access through in-person verification with your trusted inner circle.
scene7.label: Granular Privacy
scene7.sub: in-person verification — no cloud, no passwords
scene8.heading: No feeds, no profiles, no social graphs.
scene8.label: Physical Recovery
